summary: Updated the build configuration to mark Runtime/__mem.c as Matching instead of NonMatching in the Gekko runtime object list. This was a one-line status flip indicating the __mem runtime source now matched its target assembly well enough to be promoted in configure.py.
what_changed: In configure.py, within RuntimeLib("Gekko runtime", ...), changed Object(NonMatching, "Runtime/__mem.c") to Object(Matching, "Runtime/__mem.c").
No source-code diff for Runtime/__mem.c was included in the PR slice; the observable change is only the matching-status annotation in the object list.
smart_moves: Kept the change minimal and localized to the object status in configure.py, avoiding unrelated edits.

Promoted only the specific runtime object __mem.c from NonMatching to Matching, preserving the existing Matching status of neighboring runtime objects such as __va_arg.c, global_destructor_chain.c, and Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c.
decomp_lessons: Once a runtime object is verified matching, update the project object registry from NonMatching to Matching so progress tracking and build categorization reflect the current state.
For small decomp progress PRs, a narrowly scoped configure.py status flip is acceptable when the underlying file has already been made to match or verified elsewhere.
The PR provides weak evidence about the matching technique itself; no diff, comments, or review discussion explain how Runtime/__mem.c was matched.
naming_conventions: Runtime library objects are registered through Object(Matching, "Runtime/<file>.c") or Object(NonMatching, "Runtime/<file>.c") inside RuntimeLib.
The project uses the exact path casing Runtime/__mem.c for the memory runtime source.
The runtime library group name in configure.py is "Gekko runtime".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level tactics are visible in this PR slice.
The only matching tactic evidenced is changing the object classification from NonMatching to Matching after verification.
follow_up_queries: Look for earlier PRs or commits modifying Runtime/__mem.c to understand what code or matching fixes enabled this status promotion.
Search configure.py for other Runtime objects still marked NonMatching to identify adjacent runtime-library decomp targets.
Check build reports or progress scripts around February 2024 for confirmation of __mem.c matching status.
